items.title: KKR’s Private Credit Clients Slow Down Their Redemption Requests
items.description: Investors in a KKR & Co. retail private credit fund got back all of their requested cash in the second quarter, a sign that individual investors’ skittishness over the asset class may be easing.

items.title: Traders Are Most Positive on Dollar Since 2015 as Fed Hike Looms
items.description: Global traders have turned the most positive on the outlook for the dollar since 2015 as bets that borrowing costs will remain elevated for longer have fueled a monthlong rally in the US currency.

items.title: Hedge Funds Turn Most Negative on the Japanese Yen Since 2007
items.description: Hedge funds turned the most negative on the yen since 2007 as the currency trades near its weakest level in four decades.

items.description: Short sellers are anticipating more pain ahead for Hertz Global Holdings Inc. after the stock plummeted almost 60% in less than two weeks.

items.description: Lone Pine Capital posted a 43% gain for its hedge fund investors through June, notching one of its best-ever starts to a year and landing it among 2026’s top performers.

items.title: Advertising Technology Firm Criteo Attracts Vista Equity-Backed Takeover Offer
items.description: Vista Equity Partners and the investment firm Quinti Capital have teamed up on a takeover offer for French advertising-technology company Criteo SA, according to people familiar with the matter.
